title: Chole (Punjabi Chickpea Curry)

overview.description: A hearty North Indian chickpea curry with tomatoes, onions and warming spices. Rich, comforting and ideal with rice or freshly made naan.

ingredients: 1 tsp amchur (or juice of ½ lemon)
method: Drain the soaked chickpeas and place in a saucepan with fresh water.
Bring to the boil, then simmer for 60–90 minutes until tender. Drain, reserving a little cooking liquid.
Heat the oil or ghee over a medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until deeply golden.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chillies and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the coriander, cumin, Kashmiri chilli powder and turmeric. Cook for 30 seconds.
Add the tomatoes and cook for 10 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the cooked chickpeas and a splash of the reserved cooking liquid.
Simmer gently for 15 minutes, lightly crushing a few chickpeas against the side of the pan to naturally thicken the sauce.
Stir in the garam masala and amchur (or lemon juice).
Season to taste and garnish with fresh coriander.

learning.did-you-know: Chole is one of the signature dishes of Punjab and is traditionally served with bhature, a deep-fried leavened bread.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a deeply flavoured curry by browning onions well and allowing the tomatoes to cook down before adding the chickpeas.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive pantry meal that's packed with flavour and freezes beautifully, making it ideal for batch cooking.
chapman-tips: Cook the onions until they're properly golden—this is where much of the flavour comes from.
Crushing a few chickpeas naturally thickens the curry without adding flour or cornflour.
Amchur gives the authentic tang, but lemon juice is an excellent substitute.
This curry tastes even better the next day.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Serve with rice, naan or use as a filling for wraps.
